title: Contact form · datazoo.de
body:
Inbound contact-form submission from Markus Weber at Münchner Pharma — the first warm signal in the account.

> "We're evaluating shared analytical sandboxes for clinical-trial units and want to avoid per-unit Snowflake procurement."

title: Discovery call · Münchner Pharma × data zoo
body:
47-minute call. Three R&D sites on different platform configs; demand consolidation is broken between them — the primary pain. Reiter confirmed she is the budget authority. Three qualification criteria agreed.

## Transcript (excerpt)
- **00:14** Hoffmann — shop-floor visibility lags 4–24h depending on the line.
- **01:42** Reiter — I'm the budget authority; procurement runs the paper.
- **02:10** Reiter — yes to all three criteria.

title: Delivery kickoff · Münchner Pharma × data zoo
body: Kickoff after commercial close. Weber named technical sponsor; first-unit provisioning target set. Inherited obligations from the engagement spine walked through and confirmed as the delivery backlog.

title: Proposal · scope clarification on 3-site data model
body: Outbound proposal threading from the discovery criteria. Clarifies the 3-site demand-consolidation scope and restates the inherited commitments: tenant write isolation, audit trail, sub-day provisioning, and a low-engineering-burden reference case study.

title: Project status · Münchner Pharma phase-1
body: Week-9 status: first R&D unit live and accepted, second in provisioning, schedule confidence high. Notes the adjacent-integration effort that the scope-creep detector later quantifies.

title: RE: scope clarification on 3-site data model
body: Dr. Sabine Reiter replies to the proposal confirming the 3-site scope and the executive sponsorship. Flags that the third site was always assumed in-scope on their side — the seed of the later 3-site change order.

title: Scope-creep auto-detector · Münchner Pharma phase-1
body: Detector scan: 11 workitems delivered without milestone/commitment linkage; 4 represent ~€18k unbilled effort on adjacent integration. Reframe: this is a renewal signal, not a billing-recovery opportunity. Recommendation: route to engagement-strategist for renewal_skill spawn.

title: Re: phase-2 adjacent integration — budget?
body: Reiter asks whether the adjacent-integration work seen in phase-1 can be folded into a phase-2 scope. Unprocessed — awaiting an agent to fold it onto the renewal.
